headline: The Complete Calvin and Hobbes: A Last-Minute Father's Day Gift
summary: Father's Day is nearly here, making this a timely gift recommendation. "The Complete Calvin and Hobbes" compiles all of Bill Watterson's beloved comic strips. It covers the comic's entire ten-year run from 1985 through 1995.

headline: Android 17, Wear OS 7, and Android XR: New Features Revealed
summary: Android 17 introduces new "Bubble" app windows, Screen Reaction, and 50/50 gaming mode for foldables. Wear OS 7 brings Live Updates, better battery life, and prepares for Android XR smart glasses. These updates aim to enhance multitasking, smartwatch functionality, and introduce future AR/VR experiences.

headline: Android 17 Rolls Out to Pixel Phones Today
summary: Google is rolling out Android 17 to compatible Pixel phones starting today. The update includes additional exclusive features as part of the June Pixel Drop. Not every feature announced at the pre-I/O Android Show is available immediately.

headline: Wear OS 7 Launches with Live Updates and Enhanced Battery Life
summary: Google's Wear OS 7 update is rolling out for Pixel Watch 2, 3, and 4 devices. It adds a new Live Updates feature for tracking real-time events directly on your smartwatch. The update also delivers a significant boost to battery life.

headline: Housemarque Aims for Growth Trajectory Similar to FromSoftware
summary: Saros developer Housemarque expresses aspirations to match the growth of FromSoftware. FromSoftware is renowned for creating successful titles like Elden Ring. Housemarque hopes to achieve a similar trajectory in the gaming industry.

headline: Death Stranding Movie Targets 2027 Shoot, Game Characters Confirmed
summary: The Death Stranding movie is targeting a 2027 shoot date for its production. Director Michael Sarnoski recently submitted a second draft of the script. Characters from the video games are confirmed to appear in the film.

headline: Luna Abyss Dev Team Laid Off Just One Month After Launch
summary: The entire development team behind the game Luna Abyss has been laid off. This unfortunate event occurred only one month after the shooter's release. Luna Abyss was a well-received game, making the layoffs particularly surprising.

headline: Tom Holland Consulted Fans for Spider-Man: Brand New Day
summary: Tom Holland extensively researched fan desires for his role in "Spider-Man: Brand New Day." He reportedly spent hours watching YouTube and listening to podcasts. Holland aimed to find out what fans were "yearning for" in the new film.

headline: British Couple Recount Russian Warship Firing Warning Shots
summary: A British retired couple described a "surreal" incident involving a Russian warship. Warning shots were fired near their vessel in the English Channel. They had attempted to signal their changed course to the warship.

headline: Tehran Hails US Deal as Victory, Iranians See it as Necessity
summary: Tehran is portraying a recent deal with the US as a national victory. For many Iranians, however, the deal is viewed as a necessity rather than a triumph. Their primary concerns are lowering prices and reducing the fear of another war.

headline: Trump "Furious" with Netanyahu Over Lebanon Strikes
summary: US President Trump expressed fury over Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu's strikes on Lebanon. Trump called the strikes "vicious" in his public reaction. The BBC's Tom Bateman analyzes the reasons behind this significant diplomatic tension.

headline: Teens Weigh In: Social Media's Risks and Opportunities
summary: Prime Minister Keir Starmer proposes banning social media for children under 16. Teens acknowledge the risks of social media but also highlight its opportunities. They discuss how a ban might impact their "time, security and freedom to grow up."
